questions.prompt: Are you asking about your own record?
options: I am helping someone complete their own check

questions.prompt: Did this case happen in Wyoming (not a federal case)?
How did the case end?
options: Arrest or citation with no charge filed
Dismissed, no-billed, nolle prosequi, or not prosecuted
Diversion, deferred disposition, supervision, or similar program
Juvenile adjudication or offense committed as a minor

Arrest or citation with no charge filed.label: Arrested, but never charged
Arrest or citation with no charge filed.helperText: arrest or citation with no charge filed

Dismissed, no-billed, nolle prosequi, or.label: The case was dropped or thrown out
Dismissed, no-billed, nolle prosequi, or.helperText: dismissed, no-billed, nolle prosequi, or not prosecuted

Diversion, deferred disposition, supervi.label: Completed a program instead of a conviction
Diversion, deferred disposition, supervi.helperText: diversion, deferred disposition, supervision, or similar

Juvenile adjudication or offense committ.label: It happened when I was a minor
Juvenile adjudication or offense committ.helperText: juvenile adjudication or offense as a minor

I am not sure.label: I'm not sure
I am not sure.helperText: keep — and this is fine; Wilma and the tool can help

questions.prompt: What kind of charge was it?
Do any of these sound like your situation?
options: None of these / I am not sure
questions.helper: This answer is optional and does not select your legal pathway. The backend uses the factual answers and state rules.

questions.prompt: Step 1 - Adult or juvenile/minor?
Was this an adult case, or were you under 18 when it happened?
Step 2 - Conviction or no conviction?
Did the case end in conviction/guilty/no contest, or was it dismissed, acquitted, declined, or never charged?
Was this handled through deferred prosecution, first-offender treatment, probation before judgment, or a dismissal after completing probation?
Did the offense involve a gun, sex-offender registration, violence, a child victim, domestic battery, weapons, escape, bribery, or harm to a patient by a health-care provider?
When did you finish probation, court-ordered programs, and restitution?
How old were you when this happened?
Have you finished everything the court ordered?
questions.helperText: jail/probation/parole, classes, community service, anything still owed

questions.prompt: Have you paid off everything the court charged?
Do you have any open cases right now?
questions.helperText: a charge in progress, or current probation/parole

questions.prompt: Have you had a record cleared before, anywhere?
Did this happen because you were a victim of human trafficking?
Have you gotten a pardon or similar official relief for this?
When did the case end or finish?
Which court or agency handled the case?
What does the record say you were charged with?
questions.helperText: the charge name or code, however it's written

questions.prompt: Do you have your court paperwork handy?
questions.helperText: No worries if not — you can save and come back.

questions.prompt: Where in Wyoming did the case happen?
What's the case number?
questions.helperText: also called a docket, cause, or arrest number — it's on your court paperwork

caseOutcomeOptions.label: Arrest or citation with no charge filed
Dismissed, no-billed, nolle prosequi, or not prosecuted
Diversion, deferred disposition, supervision, or similar program
Juvenile adjudication or offense committed as a minor

pathways.label: Adult non-conviction expungement - W.S. 7-13-1401
pathways.summary: This is the main route for arrest-only or dismissed cases. Wilma must confirm all of these: Requirement Wyoming rule Waiting period At least 180 days have passed since arrest or dismissal Pending charges No formal charges are pending when the petition is filed Incident result No conviction from any charge related to the incident, or no charges were filed, or all proceedings were dismissed Deferred-disposition bar No disposition under W.S. 7-13-301, W.S. 35-7-1037, or former W.S. 7-13-203 from…
ruleClauses: This is the main route for arrest-only or dismissed cases.
Wilma must confirm all of these:
Waiting period At least 180 days have passed since arrest or dismissal
Pending charges No formal charges are pending when the petition is filed
Incident result No conviction from any charge related to the incident, or no charges were filed, or all proceedings were dismissed
Deferred-disposition bar No disposition under W.S. 7-13-301, W.S. 35-7-1037, or former W.S. 7-13-203 from the incident
Filing fee No filing fee under 7-13-1401
Service Verified petition served on prosecuting attorney
Objection window Prosecutor has 20 days to object
Effect Court file sealed; certified order sent to DCI; person may answer as if arrest/charges did not occur unless law says otherwise

The statute requires the 180-day wait, no pending charges, no listed deferred dispositions, and a no-conviction/no-charge/dismissal result tied to the incident. It also provides no filing fee, a 20-day prosecutor objection window, and the right to respond as if the arrest/charges did not occur after expungement. (
"https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-14/section-7-13-1401/")

Wilma trap: A Wyoming dismissal after a deferred prosecution is not automatically a 7-13-1401 expungement case. Section 7-13-1401 expressly excludes certain deferred dispositions from that non-conviction route. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-14/section-7-13-1401/")
waitingRules: Waiting period At least 180 days have passed since arrest or dismissal
Objection window Prosecutor has 20 days to object

frontendBranch.entryCondition: Backend matched this jurisdiction-specific route after evaluating the cited source rules.

pathways.label: Misdemeanor conviction expungement - W.S. 7-13-1501
pathways.summary: A person who pled guilty, pled no contest, or was convicted of a Wyoming misdemeanor may petition the convicting court for expungement of records of conviction. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1501/") Eligibility checklist
ruleClauses: A person who pled guilty, pled no contest, or was convicted of a Wyoming misdemeanor may petition the convicting court for expungement of records of conviction. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1501/")
Case type Misdemeanor guilty plea, no-contest plea, or conviction
Waiting period - status offense 1 year after expiration of sentence terms, including probation and court-ordered program completion
Waiting period - non-status offense 5 years after expiration of sentence terms, including probation and court-ordered program completion
Firearm bar Misdemeanor must not have involved use or attempted use of a firearm
Health-care provider bar Health-care provider convicted under W.S. 6-2-313 against a patient is not eligible
Danger finding Court must find petitioner is not a substantial danger to self, victim, or society
One-time limit Only one expungement under 7-13-1501
Service Verified petition served on prosecutor and DCI
Objection window Prosecutor/victim has 30 days to object

The statute sets the 5-year / 1-year timing rule, firearm bar, health-care provider bar, $100 filing fee, DCI/prosecutor service requirement, 30-day objection process, substantial-danger finding, and one-time limit. (
"https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1501/")

is conduct that would not be a crime if committed by an adult, with statutory exceptions. In plain English: it is usually something illegal because of the persons age/status, not because the conduct is criminal for everyone. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-1/section-7-1-107/?utm_source=chatgpt.com")
Wilma trap: Do not assume all underage alcohol/nicotine/minor conduct is automatically a status offense for this statute. Verify the exact charge and statute.
waitingRules: Waiting period - status offense 1 year after expiration of sentence terms, including probation and court-ordered program completion
Waiting period - non-status offense 5 years after expiration of sentence terms, including probation and court-ordered program completion
Objection window Prosecutor/victim has 30 days to object
exclusionRules: Health-care provider bar Health-care provider convicted under W.S. 6-2-313 against a patient is not eligible

frontendBranch.entryCondition: Backend matched this jurisdiction-specific route after evaluating the cited source rules.

pathways.label: Felony conviction expungement - W.S. 7-13-1502
pathways.summary: A person convicted of an eligible felony, or eligible felonies from the same occurrence or related course of events, may petition the convicting court. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1502/") Eligibility checklist
ruleClauses: A person convicted of an eligible felony, or eligible felonies from the same occurrence or related course of events, may petition the convicting court. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1502/")
Waiting period At least 10 years since sentence expiration, including probation
Court programs Any court-ordered program must be completed
Restitution Court-ordered restitution must be paid in full
Other felonies Other than the felony/felonies being expunged, petitioner must not have previously pled guilty, pled no contest, or been convicted of a felony
Firearm bar Felony must not have involved use or attempted use of a firearm, except certain Title 23 offenses
Danger finding Court must find petitioner is not a substantial danger to self, victim, or society
One-time limit Only one felony conviction expungement under this section
Service Verified petition served on prosecutor and DCI
Objection window Prosecutor/victim has 90 days to object
Effect on rights Expungement under 7-13-1502 restores rights removed because of the conviction

The statute sets the 10-year wait, restitution/program completion rules, other-felony bar, firearm bar, $300 fee, 90-day objection window, court sealing/DCI transmission, one-time limit, and restoration-of-rights language. (
"https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1502/")
Wilma should flag likely ineligible/manual review if the felony is:
a violent felony under W.S. 6-1-104(a)(xii);
aggravated homicide by vehicle;
sexual assault or related sexual offense under W.S. 6-2-301 through 6-2-320;
certain domestic battery offenses;
aggravated assault/battery on a corrections or detention officer;
sexual exploitation of children;
endangering children with controlled substances;
escape from official detention / violent or armed escape;
weapons offenses under W.S. 6-8-101 or 6-8-102;
any offense subject to sex-offender registration under W.S. 7-19-302(g) through (j). ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1502/")
waitingRules: Waiting period At least 10 years since sentence expiration, including probation
Objection window Prosecutor/victim has 90 days to object
exclusionRules: Wilma should flag likely ineligible/manual review if the felony is:

pathways.label: Human-trafficking victim vacatur - W.S. 6-2-708
pathways.summary: Wyoming has a separate victim/survivor route. A human-trafficking victim is not criminally liable for commercial sex acts or other criminal acts committed as a direct result of, or incident to, being a trafficking victim. At any time after conviction, the court that entered the conviction may vacate it if the persons participation in the offense resulted from being a victim. Official documentation creates a presumption, but is not required.…
ruleClauses: Wyoming has a separate victim/survivor route. A human-trafficking victim is not criminally liable for commercial sex acts or other criminal acts committed as a direct result of, or incident to, being a trafficking victim. At any time after conviction, the court that entered the conviction may vacate it if the persons participation in the offense resulted from being a victim. Official documentation creates a presumption, but is not required. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-6/chapter-2/article-7/section-6-2-708/")
Wilma trauma rule: Ask only what is necessary:
Was this charge connected to trafficking, coercion, exploitation, survival circumstances, or someone forcing or directing you to do something connected to the case?
Then route to manual/legal review. Do not require graphic details.

frontendBranch.entryCondition: Backend matched this jurisdiction-specific route after evaluating the cited source rules.

pathways.label: Juvenile / minor expungement - W.S. 14-6-241
pathways.summary: Wyoming juvenile/minor expungement is separate from adult expungement. Eligible categories include:
ruleClauses: Wyoming juvenile/minor expungement is separate from adult expungement.
Juvenile/minor record Court route
Juvenile delinquency adjudication other than violent felony Juvenile court petition after age of majority
Minor municipal ordinance conviction Municipal court petition
Minor misdemeanor conviction in circuit court Circuit court petition
Minor diversion or deferral Petition to court that ordered diversion/deferral
Minor arrest/charge/disposition ending in dismissal, declined prosecution, or no conviction/adjudication Petition to court

The statute allows a person adjudicated delinquent for a nonviolent felony-equivalent act to petition after reaching the age of majority, and also covers municipal ordinance convictions, minor misdemeanor circuit-court convictions, diversion/deferral, and non-conviction minor records. There is no filing fee for juvenile expungement petitions. (
"https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-14/chapter-6/article-2/section-14-6-241/")
person has reached age of majority;
record is not for a violent felony as defined by Wyoming law;
no felony conviction since adjudication;
no felony proceeding pending or being instituted;
rehabilitation has been attained to the satisfaction of the court or prosecuting attorney;
prosecutor was served and had the 20-day objection period. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-14/chapter-6/article-2/section-14-6-241/")

After juvenile expungement, proceedings are deemed never to have occurred, and the person may answer accordingly. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-14/chapter-6/article-2/section-14-6-241/")

frontendBranch.entryCondition: Backend matched this jurisdiction-specific route after evaluating the cited source rules.

when.sourceConditionText: Successfully completed felony deferred prosecution Usually not eligible under felony conviction expungement Wyoming Supreme Court held 7-13-1502 applies to people with felony convictions; a completed deferral dismissed without conviction is not a felony conviction
Arrest, no charges filed W.S. 7-13-1401 Possible after 180 days, if no pending charges and no disqualifying deferred disposition

Wyoming has real expungement, but adult expungement mainly seals/removes records from public dissemination.No-conviction route = 7-13-1401, 180 days, no pending charges, no disqualifying deferral.Misdemeanor conviction route = 7-13-1501, 1 year for status offenses, 5 years for non-status offenses, no firearm, one-time only.Felony conviction route = 7-13-1502, 10 years after completion/restitution, no other felony, no firearm, major exclusions, one-time only.Felony deferred prosecution is not treated as a felony conviction expungement route after Lynch.Juvenile/minor route = 14-6-241, stronger destruction/deletion language, no filing fee.Human-trafficking victim route = 6-2-708 vacatur, available any time after conviction if participation resulted from victimization.
Wyoming does not automatically expunge most records. The Judicial Branch says users should not expect automatic expungement and must file a petition with the proper court. (
Charges dismissed / acquitted / no conviction W.S. 7-13-1401 Possible after 180 days from arrest or dismissal, if all charges from the incident ended without conviction
Guilty plea / no-contest plea / misdemeanor conviction W.S. 7-13-1501 Possible after 1 year for status offenses or 5 years for non-status offenses, if no firearm involvement and other rules satisfied
Felony conviction W.S. 7-13-1502 Possible after 10 years from sentence/program/restitution completion, if felony is not excluded

Juvenile delinquency / minor municipal / minor misdemeanor / juvenile diversion or deferral W.S. 14-6-241 Possible after age of majority, if no later felony, no pending felony case, and rehabilitation shown
Human-trafficking victim conviction W.S. 6-2-708 vacatur Court may vacate conviction at any time if participation resulted from trafficking victimization
Pardon / innocence issue Separate pardon or innocence route Not the normal expungement pathway; manual review
"https://www.wyocourts.gov/legal-help-by-topic/expungements/")
Adult arrest/no charge/dismissal/acquittal 180 days after arrest or dismissal
Adult misdemeanor status offense 1 year after sentence/probation/program completion
Adult misdemeanor non-status offense 5 years after sentence/probation/program completion
Adult felony conviction 10 years after sentence/probation/program completion and full restitution payment
Juvenile delinquency/minor record After reaching age of majority
Human-trafficking victim vacatur Any time after conviction
Adult non-conviction expungement - W.S. 7-13-1401
Misdemeanor conviction expungement - W.S. 7-13-1501
Felony conviction expungement - W.S. 7-13-1502
Human-trafficking victim vacatur - W.S. 6-2-708
Juvenile / minor expungement - W.S. 14-6-241
No source-defined jurisdiction pathway matched the submitted facts.

waitingPeriodRules.ruleText: Route Waiting period / timing Adult arrest/no charge/dismissal/acquittal 180 days after arrest or dismissal Adult misdemeanor status offense 1 year after sentence/probation/program completion Adult misdemeanor non-status offense 5 years after sentence/probation/program completion Adult felony conviction 10 years after sentence/probation/program completion and full restitution payment Juvenile delinquency/minor record After reaching age of majority Human-trafficking victim vacatur Any time after conviction Prosecutor objection - 7-13-1401 20 days Prosecutor/victim objection - misdemeanor 30 days Prosecutor/victim objection - felony 90 days

waitingPeriodRules.ruleText: Wyoming has real expungement, but adult expungement mainly seals/removes records from public dissemination.No-conviction route = 7-13-1401, 180 days, no pending charges, no disqualifying deferral.Misdemeanor conviction route = 7-13-1501, 1 year for status offenses, 5 years for non-status offenses, no firearm, one-time only.Felony conviction route = 7-13-1502, 10 years after completion/restitution, no other felony, no firearm, major exclusions, one-time only.Felony deferred prosecution is not treated as a felony conviction expungement route after Lynch.Juvenile/minor route = 14-6-241, stronger destruction/deletion language, no filing fee.Human-trafficking victim route = 6-2-708 vacatur, available any time after conviction if participation resulted from victimization.

For Wyoming, train Wilma with this core logic: Wyoming has real expungement, but adult expungement mainly seals/removes records from public dissemination.No-conviction route = 7-13-1401, 180 days, no pending charges, no disqualifying deferral.Misdemeanor conviction route = 7-13-1501, 1 year for status offenses, 5 years for non-status offenses, no firearm, one-time only.Felony conviction route = 7-13-1502, 10 years after completion/restitution, no other felony, no firearm, major exclusions, one-time only.Felony deferred prosecution is not treated as a felony conviction expungement route after Lynch.Juvenile/minor route = 14-6-241, stronger destruction/deletion language, no filing fee.Human-trafficking victim route = 6-2-708 vacatur, available any time after conviction if participation resulted from victimization.

sourceFormStatements: Wyomings statewide self-help page does not appear to provide one complete mandatory petition packet; it lists an Expungement Handout and explains that the person prepares a Petition for Expungement and, if granted, an Order for Expungement for the judges signature. ("https://www.wyocourts.gov/legal-help-by-topic/expungements/")
Adult non-conviction Petition for Expungement under W.S. 7-13-1401 + proposed Order for Expungement
Adult misdemeanor conviction Petition for Expungement of Records of Conviction under W.S. 7-13-1501 + proposed order
Adult felony conviction Petition for Expungement of Records of Conviction under W.S. 7-13-1502 + proposed order
Juvenile/minor record Petition for Expungement under W.S. 14-6-241 + proposed order
Human-trafficking victim relief Motion to Vacate Conviction under W.S. 6-2-708
Agent caution: County clerks, municipal courts, and circuit courts may have local templates. Wilma should not invent a statewide form number unless a specific court provides one.

Wyomings statewide self-help page does not appear to provide one complete mandatory petition packet; it lists an Expungement Handout and explains that the person prepares a Petition for Expungement and, if granted, an Order for Expungement for the judges signature. ("https://www.wyocourts.gov/legal-help-by-topic/expungements/") Wilma should look for: Route Document name Adult non-conviction Petition for Expungement under W.S.
7-13-1401 + proposed Order for Expungement Adult misdemeanor conviction Petition for Expungement of Records of Conviction under W.S.
7-13-1501 + proposed order Adult felony conviction Petition for Expungement of Records of Conviction under W.S.
7-13-1502 + proposed order Juvenile/minor record Petition for Expungement under W.S.
14-6-241 + proposed order Human-trafficking victim relief Motion to Vacate Conviction under W.S.
Wilma should not invent a statewide form number unless a specific court provides one.
attachments: The court places the file under seal, sends the certified order to DCI, and related arrest/charge/disposition records in the state central repository are removed from public access. Wyoming Judicial Branch says the petitioner should have a proposed Order for Expungement ready for the judges signature. ("https://www.wyocourts.gov/legal-help-by-topic/expungements/")

If no objection is filed, the court may enter the order summarily if eligibility is shown. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1502/") After order is granted The court places the file under seal, sends the certified order to DCI, and related arrest/charge/disposition records in the state central repository are removed from public access.
filingDestinationRules: File a verified Petition for Expungement in the court where the proceeding occurred or would have occurred. Serve the prosecuting attorney. No order may issue until 20 days after service; if the prosecutor objects, the court sets a hearing; if no objection is filed, the court may enter the order summarily if eligibility is shown. There is no filing fee. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-14/section-7-13-1401/")

File a verified petition in the convicting court. Serve the prosecuting attorney and the Division of Criminal Investigation. Pay the $100 filing fee. Prosecutor must notify identifiable victims. If prosecutor or victim objects within 30 days, or if the petitioner objects to a DCI report requested by the court, the court sets a hearing. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1501/")

File a verified petition in the convicting court. Serve the prosecuting attorney and DCI. Pay the $300 filing fee. Prosecutor must notify identifiable victims. Objection window is 90 days. If no objection is filed, the court may enter the order summarily if eligibility is shown. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1502/")

The court places the file under seal, sends the certified order to DCI, and related arrest/charge/disposition records in the state central repository are removed from public access. Wyoming Judicial Branch says the petitioner should have a proposed Order for Expungement ready for the judges signature. ("https://www.wyocourts.gov/legal-help-by-topic/expungements/")
Adult non-conviction - 7-13-1401 File a verified Petition for Expungement in the court where the proceeding occurred or would have occurred.
No order may issue until 20 days after service; if the prosecutor objects, the court sets a hearing; if no objection is filed, the court may enter the order summarily if eligibility is shown.
There is no filing fee. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-14/section-7-13-1401/") Adult misdemeanor - 7-13-1501 File a verified petition in the convicting court.

If prosecutor or victim objects within 30 days, or if the petitioner objects to a DCI report requested by the court, the court sets a hearing. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1501/") Adult felony - 7-13-1502 File a verified petition in the convicting court.

If no objection is filed, the court may enter the order summarily if eligibility is shown. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1502/") After order is granted The court places the file under seal, sends the certified order to DCI, and related arrest/charge/disposition records in the state central repository are removed from public access.

There is no filing fee. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-14/section-7-13-1401/")

Route Fee Adult non-conviction under 7-13-1401 $0 Adult misdemeanor conviction under 7-13-1501 $100 Adult felony conviction under 7-13-1502 $300 Juvenile / minor under 14-6-241 $0 Human-trafficking victim vacatur Verify with clerk; statute is a motion route, not a standard expungement filing-fee route

There is no filing fee. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-14/section-7-13-1401/")

copyGuardrails: 6-2-708 vacatur Court may vacate conviction at any time if participation resulted from trafficking victimization Pardon / innocence issue Separate pardon or innocence route Not the normal expungement pathway; manual review Wyoming does not automatically expunge most records.
The Judicial Branch says users should not expect automatic expungement and must file a petition with the proper court. ( "https://www.wyocourts.gov/legal-help-by-topic/expungements/")

It does not include local, state, or federal investigatory files used solely for criminal justice purposes. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-14/section-7-13-1401/") Juvenile expungement Juvenile expungement is stronger.

14-6-241, juvenile expungement means permanently destroying or deleting physical and electronic records, documents, and images; if the minors name appears in a broader list or index, the name must be obliterated/redacted. Use Wyoming-specific remedy terminology returned by the profile; do not relabel every remedy as expungement.
Use “may qualify,” “possible path,” and “the court or agency makes the final decision.”
Do not promise erasure, sealing, eligibility, timing, filing acceptance, or a court outcome.

sourceSections.title: How Wilma should route Wyoming cases
sourceSections.text:
Users case outcome First Wilma route Wyoming treatment
Arrest, no charges filed W.S. 7-13-1401 Possible after 180 days, if no pending charges and no disqualifying deferred disposition
Charges dismissed / acquitted / no conviction W.S. 7-13-1401 Possible after 180 days from arrest or dismissal, if all charges from the incident ended without conviction
Guilty plea / no-contest plea / misdemeanor conviction W.S. 7-13-1501 Possible after 1 year for status offenses or 5 years for non-status offenses, if no firearm involvement and other rules satisfied
Felony conviction W.S. 7-13-1502 Possible after 10 years from sentence/program/restitution completion, if felony is not excluded
Successfully completed felony deferred prosecution Usually not eligible under felony conviction expungement Wyoming Supreme Court held 7-13-1502 applies to people with felony convictions; a completed deferral dismissed without conviction is not a felony conviction
Juvenile delinquency / minor municipal / minor misdemeanor / juvenile diversion or deferral W.S. 14-6-241 Possible after age of majority, if no later felony, no pending felony case, and rehabilitation shown
Human-trafficking victim conviction W.S. 6-2-708 vacatur Court may vacate conviction at any time if participation resulted from trafficking victimization
Pardon / innocence issue Separate pardon or innocence route Not the normal expungement pathway; manual review
Wyoming does not automatically expunge most records. The Judicial Branch says users should not expect automatic expungement and must file a petition with the proper court. (

"https://www.wyocourts.gov/legal-help-by-topic/expungements/")

sourceSections.title: Remedy definitions Wilma must understand
sourceSections.text:
Adult expungement
For adult records under W.S. 7-13-1401, Wyoming defines expungement as classifying the record at the Division of Criminal Investigation so it is not available for dissemination except to state or federal criminal justice agencies for criminal justice purposes. It does not include local, state, or federal investigatory files used solely for criminal justice purposes. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-14/section-7-13-1401/")
Juvenile expungement
Juvenile expungement is stronger. Under W.S. 14-6-241, juvenile expungement means permanently destroying or deleting physical and electronic records, documents, and images; if the minors name appears in a broader list or index, the name must be obliterated/redacted. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-14/chapter-6/article-2/section-14-6-241/")
Wilma rule: For Wyoming adults, say
sealed from public access / removed from public dissemination,
not
destroyed everywhere.
For juveniles, Wyoming uses stronger destruction/deletion language.

sourceSections.title: Official forms / filing documents Wilma should know
sourceSections.text:
Wyomings statewide self-help page does not appear to provide one complete mandatory petition packet; it lists an Expungement Handout and explains that the person prepares a Petition for Expungement and, if granted, an Order for Expungement for the judges signature. ("https://www.wyocourts.gov/legal-help-by-topic/expungements/")
Wilma should look for:

Route Document name
Adult non-conviction Petition for Expungement under W.S. 7-13-1401 + proposed Order for Expungement
Adult misdemeanor conviction Petition for Expungement of Records of Conviction under W.S. 7-13-1501 + proposed order
Adult felony conviction Petition for Expungement of Records of Conviction under W.S. 7-13-1502 + proposed order
Juvenile/minor record Petition for Expungement under W.S. 14-6-241 + proposed order
Human-trafficking victim relief Motion to Vacate Conviction under W.S. 6-2-708
Agent caution: County clerks, municipal courts, and circuit courts may have local templates. Wilma should not invent a statewide form number unless a specific court provides one.

sourceSections.title: Adult non-conviction expungement - W.S. 7-13-1401
sourceSections.text:
This is the main route for arrest-only or dismissed cases.
Wilma must confirm all of these:
Requirement Wyoming rule
Waiting period At least 180 days have passed since arrest or dismissal
Pending charges No formal charges are pending when the petition is filed
Incident result No conviction from any charge related to the incident, or no charges were filed, or all proceedings were dismissed
Deferred-disposition bar No disposition under W.S. 7-13-301, W.S. 35-7-1037, or former W.S. 7-13-203 from the incident
Filing fee No filing fee under 7-13-1401
Service Verified petition served on prosecuting attorney
Objection window Prosecutor has 20 days to object
Effect Court file sealed; certified order sent to DCI; person may answer as if arrest/charges did not occur unless law says otherwise
The statute requires the 180-day wait, no pending charges, no listed deferred dispositions, and a no-conviction/no-charge/dismissal result tied to the incident. It also provides no filing fee, a 20-day prosecutor objection window, and the right to respond as if the arrest/charges did not occur after expungement. (

"https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-14/section-7-13-1401/")
Wilma trap: A Wyoming dismissal after a deferred prosecution is not automatically a 7-13-1401 expungement case. Section 7-13-1401 expressly excludes certain deferred dispositions from that non-conviction route. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-14/section-7-13-1401/")

sourceSections.title: Misdemeanor conviction expungement - W.S. 7-13-1501
sourceSections.text:
A person who pled guilty, pled no contest, or was convicted of a Wyoming misdemeanor may petition the convicting court for expungement of records of conviction. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1501/")
Eligibility checklist

Requirement Rule
Case type Misdemeanor guilty plea, no-contest plea, or conviction
Waiting period - status offense 1 year after expiration of sentence terms, including probation and court-ordered program completion
Waiting period - non-status offense 5 years after expiration of sentence terms, including probation and court-ordered program completion
Firearm bar Misdemeanor must not have involved use or attempted use of a firearm
Health-care provider bar Health-care provider convicted under W.S. 6-2-313 against a patient is not eligible
Danger finding Court must find petitioner is not a substantial danger to self, victim, or society
One-time limit Only one expungement under 7-13-1501
Filing fee $100
Service Verified petition served on prosecutor and DCI
Objection window Prosecutor/victim has 30 days to object
The statute sets the 5-year / 1-year timing rule, firearm bar, health-care provider bar, $100 filing fee, DCI/prosecutor service requirement, 30-day objection process, substantial-danger finding, and one-time limit. (

"https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1501/")
Status offense definition
A
status offense
is conduct that would not be a crime if committed by an adult, with statutory exceptions. In plain English: it is usually something illegal because of the persons age/status, not because the conduct is criminal for everyone. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-1/section-7-1-107/?utm_source=chatgpt.com")
Wilma trap: Do not assume all underage alcohol/nicotine/minor conduct is automatically a status offense for this statute. Verify the exact charge and statute.

sourceSections.title: Felony conviction expungement - W.S. 7-13-1502
Human-trafficking victim vacatur - W.S. 6-2-708
sourceSections.text:
Wyoming has a separate victim/survivor route. A human-trafficking victim is not criminally liable for commercial sex acts or other criminal acts committed as a direct result of, or incident to, being a trafficking victim. At any time after conviction, the court that entered the conviction may vacate it if the persons participation in the offense resulted from being a victim. Official documentation creates a presumption, but is not required. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-6/chapter-2/article-7/section-6-2-708/")
Wilma trauma rule: Ask only what is necessary:

Was this charge connected to trafficking, coercion, exploitation, survival circumstances, or someone forcing or directing you to do something connected to the case?

Then route to manual/legal review. Do not require graphic details.

sourceSections.title: Juvenile / minor expungement - W.S. 14-6-241
sourceSections.text:
Wyoming juvenile/minor expungement is separate from adult expungement.
Eligible categories include:

Juvenile/minor record Court route
Juvenile delinquency adjudication other than violent felony Juvenile court petition after age of majority
Minor municipal ordinance conviction Municipal court petition
Minor misdemeanor conviction in circuit court Circuit court petition
Minor diversion or deferral Petition to court that ordered diversion/deferral
Minor arrest/charge/disposition ending in dismissal, declined prosecution, or no conviction/adjudication Petition to court
The statute allows a person adjudicated delinquent for a nonviolent felony-equivalent act to petition after reaching the age of majority, and also covers municipal ordinance convictions, minor misdemeanor circuit-court convictions, diversion/deferral, and non-conviction minor records. There is no filing fee for juvenile expungement petitions. (

"https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-14/chapter-6/article-2/section-14-6-241/")
Juvenile requirements
Wilma must confirm:
person has reached age of majority;
record is not for a violent felony as defined by Wyoming law;
no felony conviction since adjudication;
no felony proceeding pending or being instituted;
rehabilitation has been attained to the satisfaction of the court or prosecuting attorney;
prosecutor was served and had the 20-day objection period. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-14/chapter-6/article-2/section-14-6-241/")
After juvenile expungement, proceedings are deemed never to have occurred, and the person may answer accordingly. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-14/chapter-6/article-2/section-14-6-241/")

sourceSections.title: Filing instructions
sourceSections.text:
Adult non-conviction - 7-13-1401
File a verified Petition for Expungement in the court where the proceeding occurred or would have occurred. Serve the prosecuting attorney. No order may issue until 20 days after service; if the prosecutor objects, the court sets a hearing; if no objection is filed, the court may enter the order summarily if eligibility is shown. There is no filing fee. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-14/section-7-13-1401/")
Adult misdemeanor - 7-13-1501
File a verified petition in the convicting court. Serve the prosecuting attorney and the Division of Criminal Investigation. Pay the $100 filing fee. Prosecutor must notify identifiable victims. If prosecutor or victim objects within 30 days, or if the petitioner objects to a DCI report requested by the court, the court sets a hearing. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1501/")
Adult felony - 7-13-1502
File a verified petition in the convicting court. Serve the prosecuting attorney and DCI. Pay the $300 filing fee. Prosecutor must notify identifiable victims. Objection window is 90 days. If no objection is filed, the court may enter the order summarily if eligibility is shown. ("https://law.justia.com/codes/wyoming/title-7/chapter-13/article-15/section-7-13-1502/")
After order is granted
The court places the file under seal, sends the certified order to DCI, and related arrest/charge/disposition records in the state central repository are removed from public access. Wyoming Judicial Branch says the petitioner should have a proposed Order for Expungement ready for the judges signature. ("https://www.wyocourts.gov/legal-help-by-topic/expungements/")

sourceSections.title: Fees
sourceSections.text:
Route Fee
Adult non-conviction under 7-13-1401 $0
Adult misdemeanor conviction under 7-13-1501 $100
Adult felony conviction under 7-13-1502 $300
Juvenile / minor under 14-6-241 $0
Human-trafficking victim vacatur Verify with clerk; statute is a motion route, not a standard expungement filing-fee route

sourceSections.title: Record request process
Waiting-period table
sourceSections.text:
Route Waiting period / timing
Adult arrest/no charge/dismissal/acquittal 180 days after arrest or dismissal
Adult misdemeanor status offense 1 year after sentence/probation/program completion
Adult misdemeanor non-status offense 5 years after sentence/probation/program completion
Adult felony conviction 10 years after sentence/probation/program completion and full restitution payment
Juvenile delinquency/minor record After reaching age of majority
Human-trafficking victim vacatur Any time after conviction
Prosecutor objection - 7-13-1401 20 days
Prosecutor/victim objection - misdemeanor 30 days
Prosecutor/victim objection - felony 90 days

sourceSections.title: Wilma intake decision tree
Agent-facing summary
sourceSections.text:
For Wyoming, train Wilma with this core logic:
Wyoming has real expungement, but adult expungement mainly seals/removes records from public dissemination.No-conviction route = 7-13-1401, 180 days, no pending charges, no disqualifying deferral.Misdemeanor conviction route = 7-13-1501, 1 year for status offenses, 5 years for non-status offenses, no firearm, one-time only.Felony conviction route = 7-13-1502, 10 years after completion/restitution, no other felony, no firearm, major exclusions, one-time only.Felony deferred prosecution is not treated as a felony conviction expungement route after Lynch.Juvenile/minor route = 14-6-241, stronger destruction/deletion language, no filing fee.Human-trafficking victim route = 6-2-708 vacatur, available any time after conviction if participation resulted from victimization.

render a paid packet action for an automatic, guidance-only, waiting, missing-info, review, ineligible, or hard-stop result
